reason: The text explicitly demonstrates Moses's humility through his response to God's commission. When God calls him to lead the Israelites out of Egypt, Moses responds with the quintessential expression of humility: 'מִי אָנֹכִי' (Who am I?), questioning his own worthiness and capability for such a great task. Additionally, the narrative depicts him as a shepherd (a humble occupation), and shows him covering his face in fear and reverence when God speaks to him, unable even to look upon God. These elements collectively establish Moses as a humble figure who recognizes both his own inadequacy and God's transcendence.
